common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this?

MEAT.SMALL_CAFE: Local butcher or wholesaler; cash&carry for frozen.
MEAT.MID_RESTAURANT: Approved distributor contract + local butcher for fresh.

PRODUCE.SMALL_CAFE: Local greengrocer or market; buy fresh daily.
PRODUCE.MID_RESTAURANT: Planned + spot buying from the market, fixed days.

DRY_GOODS.SMALL_CAFE: Monthly bulk buy from cash&carry (Bizim Toptan/Tespo).
DRY_GOODS.MULTI_BRANCH: Central distributor + bulk online B2B ordering.

DAIRY.SMALL_CAFE: Cash&carry + a trusted local dairy distributor.
DAIRY.MID_RESTAURANT: Contracted, regular supply from an approved dairy distributor.

BEVERAGE.SMALL_CAFE: Bulk buy from cash&carry/Tespo with cash discounts.
BEVERAGE.MULTI_BRANCH: Central framework agreement with the brand distributor.

PACKAGING.SMALL_CAFE: Bulk buy from cash&carry to lower unit cost.

CLEANING.SMALL_CAFE: Bulk buy cleaning supplies from cash&carry.
CLEANING.MULTI_BRANCH: One cleaning supplier for all branches.

CASH_CARRY.advantage: Wide range in one stop, orderly invoicing; discounts are single-digit (~3-7%).
CASH_CARRY.minOrder: Individual membership possible; delivery usually needs a 1,500-2,500 TRY minimum basket + logistics fee.
CASH_CARRY.payment: Loyalty points (e.g. Bizim Kart); no deferred-payment/installment advantage is claimed.
CASH_CARRY.eInvoice: Check the supplier's e-Invoice registration via GİB to secure the input-VAT deduction.

WHOLESALE_MARKET.advantage: Spot pricing and wide variety for fresh produce; trading inside the market halves the levy.
WHOLESALE_MARKET.minOrder: No minimum order; a broker's commission is capped at 8%.
WHOLESALE_MARKET.payment: Usually cash/spot payment; the buyer pays the market levy.
WHOLESALE_MARKET.eInvoice: Buying directly from a producer requires registering as a notifier in the Market Registry plus a producer receipt.

ONLINE_B2B.advantage: Price transparency and a multi-temperature (fresh+frozen+dry) range; fast delivery.
ONLINE_B2B.minOrder: Varies by platform: some have no minimum, others waive shipping above 2,500 TRY.
ONLINE_B2B.payment: Account-based catalogs (e.g. Bidfood) may require membership; some platforms show prices to everyone.
ONLINE_B2B.eInvoice: Corporate B2B platforms are generally e-Invoice registered; confirm before ordering.

PRODUCER_COOP.advantage: Buying from a ministry-registered producer organization is fully exempt from the market levy and cuts out middleman margin.
PRODUCER_COOP.minOrder: Usually needs high volume/a standing agreement; not practical for a small cafe.
PRODUCER_COOP.payment: Works on a contracted/framework basis; gives price predictability at high volume.
PRODUCER_COOP.eInvoice: Documented by invoice/producer receipt; the business may still need Market Registry registration.

LOCAL_BUTCHER_WHOLESALER.advantage: Trust and flexibility (small quantities, fast delivery); preferred for fresh product.
LOCAL_BUTCHER_WHOLESALER.minOrder: Usually no minimum-order requirement; phone ordering is common.
LOCAL_BUTCHER_WHOLESALER.payment: In a professional relationship, a price quote/contract plus a supplier scorecard is recommended.
LOCAL_BUTCHER_WHOLESALER.eInvoice: The supplier must be under the official approval/registration regime; ISO 22000 certification should be expected.

DISTRIBUTOR.advantage: Contracted/framework agreements give price predictability; multi-branch uses a hybrid central+branch-local model.
DISTRIBUTOR.minOrder: Requires a corporate account/contract with the distributor; minimum order varies by supplier.
DISTRIBUTOR.payment: Contracted/framework buying gives price predictability; payment terms are negotiated with the supplier.
DISTRIBUTOR.eInvoice: Corporate distributors are e-Invoice registered; confirm via a GİB lookup.

MEAT.1: Keep the cold chain: raw meat/poultry 0…+4°C, fish 0…+2°C, frozen -18°C.
MEAT.2: Require the supplier's official approval/registration and ISO 22000 certification.
MEAT.3: At high volume, use price quotes plus a supplier scorecard; slaughterhouse traceability becomes camera-mandatory in 2027.

PRODUCE.1: The market levy is 1% inside the wholesale market, 2% outside; the buyer pays it.
PRODUCE.2: Buying directly from a producer requires notifier registration in the Market Registry; unregistered trading draws a 25% penalty levy.
PRODUCE.3: The producer-organization/co-op channel is levy-exempt and cuts middleman margin at high volume.

DRY_GOODS.1: Cash&carry's tiered discounts are single-digit (~3-7%); the real win is the wide range plus orderly invoicing.
DRY_GOODS.2: For long-shelf-life items, bulk/framework buying is the clearest win.
DRY_GOODS.3: Online B2B price transparency suits small operators; account-based catalogs suit larger scale.

DAIRY.1: The cold chain must stay unbroken below +4°C; the supplier needs refrigerated vehicles/storage.
DAIRY.2: Dairy/meat suppliers are under the stricter 'approval' regime, not just registration.
DAIRY.3: In a high food-inflation environment, contracted/framework buying gives price predictability.

BEVERAGE.1: Processed/excise-taxed drinks (soda, juice, beer) carry 10% VAT — higher than staple food.
BEVERAGE.2: Cash&carry may offer a cash/early-payment discount, but its depth is limited (~3-7%).
BEVERAGE.3: A framework agreement with a brand distributor gives price stability at high volume.

PACKAGING.1: Bulk buying can cut unit price 20-40%; paper hygiene items can save up to 30%.
PACKAGING.2: Long shelf life means 1-2 orders a month is enough; a large stock buffer is fine.
PACKAGING.3: For packaging touching food, require a food-safety certificate and store it separately from food.

CLEANING.1: Store cleaning supplies separately from food; require a certificate for food-contact-surface products.
CLEANING.2: Long shelf life means once a month ordering is enough.
CLEANING.3: Trade-association membership (TÜRES/TURYİD/ETÜDER) can unlock supplier campaigns and special discounts.
